---
name: edge-re-build
description: >-
  Investigate Edge build failures, autoninja issues, and Remote Execution infrastructure.
  Use when: "execute autoninja" pipeline step fails, autoninja errors, siso client/server issues,
  RE architecture questions, or debugging build problems on Preprod/Prod RE.
---

# Edge Remote Execution & Build Expert

Use this skill when investigating Edge build failures, autoninja issues, siso problems, or working on Remote Execution infrastructure.

## When to Use

- **"execute autoninja" pipeline failures** — most common trigger
- **autoninja errors** — local or CI build issues
- Debugging siso client or server issues
- Understanding RE architecture
- Reviewing changes to RE code in `es*/RE`
- Troubleshooting AKS/infrastructure issues

## Investigating "execute autoninja" Pipeline Failures

When a pipeline fails at the "execute autoninja" step:

### 1. Download Pipeline Artifacts (Build Logs)

Reference the pipeline* skills.
The build logs are **output from the siso client** and contain detailed information about what failed. Download these artifacts from the pipeline run:

| Artifact Name | Contents |
|---------------|----------|
| `BuildLogs-<config>-<basename>-<jobid>` | `autoninja.log` (full build output), `.ninja_log` |
| `siso-report-<config>-<basename>-<jobid>` | Detailed siso metrics and execution report (`.tgz`) |

These are published by `bash-build-edge.yml` template (see `sealion/pipelines/templates/bash-build-edge.yml`).

**Important:** To fully understand the log output, you may need to check the **siso client code** in `chromium.infra.build` — the logs are generated by siso and understanding the client behavior helps interpret errors.

### 2. Check Code Repositories

| Repository | What to Check |
|------------|---------------|
| **chromium.src** (`edge/src`) | Source compilation errors, build config issues |
| **edgeinternal.es/RE** (`es*/RE/*`) | RE backend/server-side issues & RE pipelines |
| **chromium.infra.build** (`mirror/main` branch) | Siso client code — needed to understand log output and client-side behavior |

## Siso Client Code Location

The siso code is in **chromium.infra.build** repository (use `mirror/main` branch):

```
C:\dev\chromium.infra.build\   (or /workspace/chromium.infra.build on Linux)
├── siso/                      # Siso - the build tool (Go)
│   ├── main.go                # Entry point
│   ├── auth/                  # Authentication
│   ├── build/                 # Build orchestration
│   ├── execute/               # Execution logic
│   ├── hashfs/                # Hash filesystem
│   ├── reapi/                 # Remote Execution API (gRPC calls to RE backend)
│   ├── scandeps/              # Dependency scanning
│   ├── subcmd/                # CLI subcommands
│   └── ...
├── infra/                     # Infrastructure tooling
├── kajiya/                    # Related tooling
└── ...
```

## RE Backend Code Location

The Edge RE backend is in **edgeinternal.es** repository:

```
C:\dev\es2\RE\msRemoteExecution\   (or es, es3 for parallel dev)
├── msRemoteExecution/             # ASP.NET Core host
├── msRemoteExecution.Common/      # Shared library (business logic)
│   ├── Services/                  # gRPC service implementations
│   ├── Builder/                   # Execution orchestration
│   ├── ExecuteHelpers/            # Process execution (ExecuteStage.cs)
│   ├── CAS/                       # Content Addressable Storage
│   └── FileSystemHelpers/         # Path translation
└── msRemoteExecution.Tests/       # Unit tests
```

## Key Components

| Component | Location | Purpose |
|-----------|----------|---------|
| Siso client | `chromium.infra.build/siso/` | Sends build actions to RE |
| ExecutionService | `msRemoteExecution.Common/Services/` | gRPC endpoint for Execute RPC |
| ExecuteStage | `msRemoteExecution.Common/ExecuteHelpers/` | Runs clang/python on workers |
| InputCacheManager | `msRemoteExecution.Common/CAS/` | Materializes input files |

## RE Request Flow

```
Siso Client (ninja)
    │
    ▼
ExecutionService.Execute(ExecuteRequest)
    │
    ▼
RemoteExecutionOperationHandler.ExecuteAsync
    ├── Check ActionCache
    ├── Fetch Action + Command from CAS
    └── LocalExecutionOperationHandler.ExecuteAsync
            │
            ▼
        ToolsSetup → FilesSetup → ExecuteStage
            │
            ▼
        Return ExecuteResponse
```

## Debugging Tips

### Client-side (siso)
- Check `edge/src/out/siso*` for local siso logs
- Look for upload failures, connection errors, or timeout messages

## Testing RE Changes

Set up build dir (only needed if changing commits):
```bash
# On Linux
set_downstream
gclient sync -Df
autogn

# On Windows
Set-Downstream
gclient sync -Df
autogn
```

Build with Prod RE:
```bash
# On Linux
set_downstream; autoninja -C out/linux_x64_debug_developer_build chrome

# On Windows
Set-Downstream; autoninja -C out\win_x64_debug_developer_build chrome
```

Build with Preprod RE & with no-fallback (to force RE usage):
```bash
# On Linux
set_downstream; set_dev_re; set_remote_only; autoninja -C out/linux_x64_debug_developer_build chrome

# On Windows
Set-Downstream; Set-DevRE; Set-RemoteOnly; autoninja -C out\win_x64_debug_developer_build chrome
```

If no-fallback build fails with only timeouts, restart it - this is load-related, not a bug.
